# 🛒 Store-Sales-Inventory-Analysis-using-MySQL

This project focuses on analyzing a retail store database using SQL to generate meaningful business insights.
The analysis includes sales performance, customer behavior, inventory status, and employee performance using SQL queries.

The database store_db contains tables such as:

Customers

Orders

Order Items

Products

Categories

Stores

Staffs

Stocks

⚙️ Database Setup
CREATE DATABASE store_db;
USE store_db;


## 3. Functional Requirements (Task to do):

### 3.1 Sales Analysis
/*• Calculate total revenue generated by each store.*/

select 
s.store_name,
sum(oi.quantity * oi.list_price) as total_revenue
from orders o
join order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id
join stores s on o.store_id = s.store_id
group by s.store_name
order by total_revenue desc;

Ans:

<img width="278" height="76" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/787e246f-c3e7-4dff-b010-709b4581ac66" />

Insights:

* Baldwin Bikes is the dominant revenue generator, producing ~8× more revenue than Santa Cruz Bikes.

This could indicate:Higher customer traffic,Larger inventory,Better-performing sales team


/* • Identify top 5 best-selling products.*/
select 
p.product_name,
sum(oi.quantity) as total_sales
from order_items oi
join products p on oi.product_id = p.product_id
group by p.product_name
order by total_sales desc
limit 5;

Ans:

<img width="347" height="120" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d1531ac-6341-4c2e-9f4d-c7ec47c9043d" />

Insights:

* Electra brand dominates the top-selling products, indicating strong customer demand.

* Cruiser and lifestyle bikes appear highly popular.

* Customers may prefer mid-range, comfortable bikes rather than high-performance models.



/* • Calculate monthly sales trends to observe seasonality and growth.*/

select 
date_format(o.order_date,'%y-%m') as month,
sum(oi.quantity * oi.list_price) as monthly_sales
from orders o
join order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id
group by month
order by month;

Ans:

<img width="200" height="92" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d3fb0ee7-0172-40d5-9403-4ec3da7962f3" />

 Insight

* January 2016 generated 3367.99 in revenue, which indicates high customer demand at the beginning of the year.

* Sales decreased from 3367.99 in January to 858 in February.


/* • Identify Top-Selling Product per Category */
select *
from (
select 
c.category_name,
p.product_name,
sum(oi.quantity) as total_sales,
rank() over(partition by c.category_name order by sum(oi.quantity) desc) as rnk
from order_items oi
join products p on oi.product_id = p.product_id
join categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id
group by c.category_name,p.product_name
) ranked
where rnk = 1;

Ans:

<img width="479" height="141" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dfb824e8-e038-489b-af9b-f2075ece9d0f" />


insight:

* Mountain bikes appear to have strong demand in the market, making them an important revenue driver.

* Electric bikes may have lower adoption or higher price barriers, leading to fewer sales compared to other bicycle types.

* Every category has one product ranked #1 with the highest sales.



/* • List of Products with Running Total of Sales */

SELECT 
p.product_name,
SUM(oi.quantity) AS product_sales,
SUM(SUM(oi.quantity)) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity)) AS running_total_sales
FROM order_items oi
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_name
ORDER BY product_sales;

Ans:

<img width="624" height="251" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c9fed8d-c71a-4527-a50f-352d81a7320f" />

insights

✅ Insight:
* Some products generate higher demand than others, indicating differences in customer preference.

* This helps understand how each product contributes to total cumulative sales.

* A small group of high-selling products drives a large portion of total sales, which is useful for inventory and marketing strategies.


## 3.2 Customer Analysis
/* Identify customers with the highest purchases.*/
select 
customer_id,
sum(quantity * list_price) as total_spent
from orders o
join order_items oi 
on o.order_id = oi.order_id
group by customer_id
order by total_spent desc;

Ans:

<img width="203" height="146" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa7ec786-ec1d-4417-9343-59b40a07ffa9" />

insights

* The top customer spends almost double compared to the second highest customer, indicating uneven spending behavior.

* Only a small group of customers appear at the top with high purchase amounts.

* This suggests a small percentage of customers drive a large share of revenue, which aligns with the Pareto principle (80/20 rule) often seen in sales data.


/* • Find customers who have not placed any orders. */
select 
c.customer_id,
concat(c.first_name,' ',c.last_name) as customer_name
from customers c
left join orders o 
on c.customer_id = o.customer_id
where o.order_id is null;


Ans:
<img width="226" height="262" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67d62ef9-81f0-4718-9e7d-4c7507a2c89d" />

insight:

* Customers without orders represent potential revenue opportunities.

* A high number of inactive customers could indicate:

* Businesses should analyze customer conversion rates.


/* • Identify customers who have placed at least 3 orders and spent more than $ 25,000 */

select 
o.customer_id,
count(distinct o.order_id) as total_orders,
sum(oi.quantity * oi.list_price) as total_spent
from orders o
join order_items oi 
on o.order_id = oi.order_id
group by o.customer_id
having count(distinct o.order_id) >= 3
and sum(oi.quantity * oi.list_price) > 25000;


Ans:

<img width="281" height="118" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4595fb4-b094-41c5-82bb-3f779e8be4a4" />

insights:

* Customers meeting both conditions are loyal high-spending customers.

* Customers with 3+ orders show repeat buying patterns.


## 3.3 Inventory Management
/* • Identify low-stock products (below 50 items in inventory).*/
select 
product_name,
quantity
from stocks
join products using(product_id)
where quantity < 50;

Ans:

<img width="279" height="251" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e7ab8de9-5e32-4f0e-9a25-87ae38340382" />


Insight:
* These products may soon run out of stock, so the business should consider reordering them soon.

* This product is completely out of stock, meaning customers cannot currently purchase it. Immediate restocking is required to avoid lost sales.

* This suggests the dataset tracks inventory per store, and stock availability differs between locations.






## 3.4 Employee Performance Analysis
/* • Identify the top 5 employees based on order transactions handled.*/
select 
concat(s.first_name,' ',s.last_name) as staff_name,
count(o.order_id) as orders_handled
from staffs s
join orders o on s.staff_id = o.staff_id
group by staff_name
order by orders_handled desc
limit 5;

Ans:

<img width="236" height="127" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e21d2ae-1b82-4799-9d54-2ce356671006" />



insights

* She demonstrates strong performance and productivity in processing customer orders.

* Both employees contribute significantly to order processing operations and are key performers in the team.




/* Identify staff members with the highest number of orders processed */
 select staff_id, count(order_id) as total_orders
from orders
group by staff_id
order by total_orders desc;

Ans:

<img width="184" height="147" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e93b22cc-2389-439c-97fd-b73aefebe49c" />

Insight:

* Staff ID 7 handled 32 orders, which is the highest among all staff members. This employee is the top contributor to order processing.

* The top two staff members handled:32 orders 31 orders.These two employees manage a large portion of customer transactions.


 /* • Rank Staff Based on Revenue Generated */
select 
concat(s.first_name,' ',s.last_name) as staff_name,
sum(oi.quantity * oi.list_price) as revenue,
rank() over(order by sum(oi.quantity * oi.list_price) desc) as staff_rank
from staffs s
join orders o on s.staff_id = o.staff_id
join order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id
group by staff_name;

Ans:

<img width="263" height="87" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e31d25fe-2bb2-4a12-a06b-4bdc62fa0935" />

Insight:

* Venita Daniel generated the highest revenue (3597.95) and ranked 1st, making her the top-performing staff member in terms of sales.

* Marcelene Boyer ranked 2nd with revenue of 3367.99, showing strong contribution close to the top performer.

* Genna Serrano ranked 3rd with 858 in revenue, indicating lower sales performance compared to the top two employees.



## 3.5 Advanced Reporting
/* • Rank products by sales volume. */

select product_id,total_sales,
rank() over(order by total_sales desc) as product_rank
from (select product_id,sum(quantity) as total_sales
from order_items
group by product_id
) t;

Ans:

<img width="251" height="249" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e475ba0a-c9a4-4a43-a929-232f3c65b4d9" />

Insight:

* Product ID 3 ranks 1st with 40 total sales, making it the highest-selling product in terms of quantity.

* Product IDs 12 and 8 follow with 39 and 36 sales, showing strong demand and contributing significantly to overall product sales

* Some products share the same rank (e.g., product 8 and 25 ranked 3), indicating equal sales volumes among certain products.


/* • Compare Monthly Sales with Previous Month. */

select month,sales,
lag(sales) over(order by month) as previous_month_sales
from (select 
date_format(order_date,'%y-%m') as month,
sum(quantity * list_price) as sales
from orders o join order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id
group by month)  t;

Ans:

<img width="282" height="91" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a772906-8869-4b4d-aee1-46bc7e4906f5" />

Insight :

* Sales dropped significantly from 3367.99 in Jan (16-01) to 858 in Feb (16-02), indicating a sharp decline in monthly revenue.

* The NULL month generated 3597.95 in sales, suggesting some orders have missing order_date values that affect monthly analysis.

* Comparing current and previous month sales helps identify sales trends and seasonal fluctuations in business performance.



/* • Assign Row Number to Orders by Each Customer. */
select product_id,total_sales,
rank() over(order by total_sales desc) as product_rank
from (select product_id,
sum(quantity) as total_sales
from order_items
group by product_id
)  t;


Ans:

<img width="249" height="253" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4eed0381-6405-4d6f-a14a-4883c0f41b26" />

Insight:

* Product ID 3 has the highest sales with 40 units, ranking 1st, indicating it is the most popular product.

* Product IDs 12 and 8 follow with 39 and 36 units sold, showing strong customer demand.

* Some products share the same rank (e.g., 8 and 25 ranked 3), meaning they have equal sales volumes.



Using these tables, the project performs multiple analytical tasks to help businesses make data-driven decisions.










🧠 SQL Concepts Used

This project demonstrates multiple SQL concepts including:

Joins

INNER JOIN          LEFT JOIN

Aggregation Functions

SUM()                COUNT()

Window Functions

RANK()               LAG()

Grouping & Filtering

GROUP BY             HAVING

Subqueries

Date Functions         DATE_FORMAT()



## Conclusion

This project analyzes store sales and inventory data using MySQL to generate meaningful insights. By applying SQL techniques such as joins, aggregations, subqueries, and window functions, the analysis explores important areas including sales trends, customer purchasing behavior, product performance, inventory levels, and employee performance.

The results help identify top-selling products, high-value customers, low-stock inventory items, and top-performing staff members, which are critical for improving business operations. These insights can support better sales strategies, inventory management, and decision-making in a retail environment.

Overall, this project demonstrates how MySQL can be effectively used for real-world data analysis, transforming raw transactional data into actionable business insights.
